key_insight: MARLIN can infer small-molecule structures directly from tandem mass spectra without needing a ground-truth molecular formula, which is a major step toward de novo identification of novel compounds.
clinical_relevance: By improving identification of previously unseen metabolites and natural products, this approach could support earlier biomarker discovery and therapeutic lead finding from untargeted mass spectrometry data.
method: Self-supervised encoder predicts a molecular fingerprint from raw MS/MS peaks.
Block-diffusion language model generates candidate structures from the fingerprint plus measured precursor mass.
A mass-shell constraint and exact mass agreement filter keep candidates physically consistent while preserving diversity.
evidence_signal: In the NPLIB1 benchmark, MARLIN was the strongest formula-free method across exact-match accuracy, structural distance, and fingerprint similarity, and it recovered the correct molecular formula about as often as a dedicated predictor.
watch_item: Performance is benchmark-based, so real-world robustness may depend on instrument differences, noisy spectra, and how well the mass-shell and fingerprint predictions generalize to novel chemistries.
paper_title: MARLIN: De Novo Molecular Structure Elucidation from Tandem Mass Spectra without a Ground-Truth Formula
